title: Mission class — two altitude strata
description: Vehicle must operate across two altitude strata: ground-level vertiport operations (0–30 m) and U-space corridor cruise (150–300 m AGL)

title: Design range 30–150 km
description: Design range envelope covers 30–80 km metro missions and up to 150 km regional missions including energy reserves
notes: Range drives battery sizing and charging network density

title: Cruise performance 150–250 km/h TAS at 150–300 m AGL
description: Sustained cruise at 150–250 km/h true airspeed at 150–300 m above ground level in U-space corridor under normal conditions
notes: Altitude band per EASA U-space UBL class

title: VTOL noise constraint ≤80 dB(A) at vertiport boundary
description: Noise level at vertiport boundary must not exceed 80 dB(A) during vertical take-off and landing to ensure community acceptance

title: Passenger and payload envelope
description: Vehicle must accommodate single pilot or fully autonomous operation carrying 1–6 passengers with total payload 80–600 kg including baggage
notes: Pax count drives cabin layout and emergency egress

title: MTOM range 700–3175 kg SC-VTOL compliant
description: Maximum Take-Off Mass must fall within 700–3175 kg bracket as defined by EASA SC-VTOL applicability scope
notes: Upper limit corresponds to SC-VTOL Category Enhanced

title: Energy reserves — 20 min VFR hold plus diversion
description: Vehicle must carry energy reserves sufficient for 20-minute VFR holding pattern plus diversion to an alternate vertiport or landing site under current rules
notes: Reserve policy subject to regulatory evolution

title: Dispatch target — 10 flights per vehicle per day
description: Operational target of minimum 10 revenue flights per vehicle per calendar day assuming 5–10 min turnaround at vertiport
notes: Drives battery cycle life and fast-charging requirements

title: Weather minima VFR-IFR with SC-VTOL Cat 1a maturity path
description: Initial operations under VFR minima evolving to IFR capability with SC-VTOL Cat 1a precision approach as fleet matures
notes: Cat 1a enables higher dispatch rate in low-visibility conditions

title: Vertiport infrastructure footprint ≥1.5D FATO pad
description: Vertiport Final Approach and Take-Off area must have minimum diameter of 1.5 × D where D equals 10 times the outer rotor envelope of the vehicle
notes: D = 10 × outer rotor envelope per EASA vertiport specification
